---
output: github_document
---

<!-- README.md is generated from README.Rmd. Please edit that file -->

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.path = "man/figures/README-",
  out.width = "100%"
)
```

# CVXR <img src="man/figures/logo.png" width="120" align="right" />

<!-- badges: start -->
[![R-CMD-check](https://github.com/cvxgrp/CVXR/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/cvxgrp/CVXR/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml)
[![CRAN status](https://www.r-pkg.org/badges/version/CVXR)](https://cran.r-project.org/package=CVXR)
[![Downloads](https://cranlogs.r-pkg.org/badges/CVXR)](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=CVXR)
<!-- badges: end -->

CVXR is an R package for **disciplined convex optimization**. It lets
you formulate convex problems in natural mathematical syntax ---
specifying an objective and constraints using variables, parameters,
and a library of atoms with known curvature --- and then solves them
automatically via an appropriate backend solver.

Built on the [S7](https://rconsortium.github.io/S7/) object system
and designed to mirror [CVXPY](https://www.cvxpy.org/) 1.8, this
version is a complete rewrite that is **~4--5x faster** than the
previous S4-based CVXR and supports 13 solvers.

## Installation

Install the released version from CRAN:

```{r, eval = FALSE}
install.packages("CVXR")
```

Or install the development version from GitHub:

```{r, eval = FALSE}
# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("cvxgrp/CVXR")
```

## Quick example

```{r example, eval = FALSE}
library(CVXR)

# Data
set.seed(42)
n <- 50; p <- 10
X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p)
beta_true <- c(rep(1, 5), rep(0, 5))
y <- X %*% beta_true + rnorm(n, sd = 0.5)

# Problem
beta <- Variable(p)
objective <- Minimize(sum_squares(y - X %*% beta) + 0.1 * p_norm(beta, 1))
prob <- Problem(objective)

# Solve (Clarabel is the default solver)
result <- psolve(prob)
result                          # optimal value
estimated <- value(beta)        # coefficient estimates
```

## Solvers

CVXR ships with four built-in solvers and supports nine more via
optional packages:

| Solver | Package | Problem types |
|--------|---------|---------------|
| **Clarabel** | `clarabel` (built-in) | LP, QP, SOCP, SDP, ExpCone, PowCone |
| **OSQP** | `osqp` (built-in) | LP, QP |
| **SCS** | `scs` (built-in) | LP, QP, SOCP, SDP, ExpCone, PowCone |
| **HiGHS** | `highs` (built-in) | LP, MIP |
| ECOS / ECOS_BB | `ECOSolveR` | SOCP / MIP-SOCP |
| GLPK / GLPK_MI | `Rglpk` | LP / MIP-LP |
| MOSEK | `Rmosek` | LP, QP, SOCP, SDP, ExpCone, MIP |
| Gurobi | `gurobi` | LP, QP, SOCP, MIP |
| CPLEX | `Rcplex` | LP, QP, MIP |
| CVXOPT | `cccp` | LP, SOCP, SDP |
| PIQP | `piqp` | LP, QP |

```{r, eval = FALSE}
installed_solvers()             # list available solvers
psolve(prob, solver = "OSQP")   # choose a specific solver
```

## Features

- **DCP verification** --- problems are checked for convexity at
  construction time.
- **DPP (Disciplined Parameterized Programming)** --- compile once,
  re-solve efficiently when only `Parameter()` values change.
- **DGP (Disciplined Geometric Programming)** and **DQCP (Disciplined
  Quasiconvex Programming)** extensions.
- **Complex variables** via `Variable(n, complex = TRUE)`.
- **Mixed-integer** programming with `Variable(n, boolean = TRUE)` or
  `Variable(n, integer = TRUE)`.
- **Warm-start** for 7 of 13 solvers.
- **50+ atoms** covering LP, QP, SOCP, SDP, exponential, and power
  cone problems.
- **`visualize()`** for problem introspection (text and interactive
  HTML output).

## Documentation

- **Tutorials and examples**: <https://cvxr.rbind.io>
- **Package reference**: <https://www.cvxgrp.org/CVXR/>
- **Paper**: Fu, Narasimhan, and Boyd (2020). "CVXR: An R Package for
  Disciplined Convex Optimization." *Journal of Statistical Software*,
  94(14), 1--34. [doi:10.18637/jss.v094.i14](https://doi.org/10.18637/jss.v094.i14)

If you use CVXR in your work, please cite the paper above
(`citation("CVXR")`).

## License

Apache License 2.0
